lesson: A frozen block with the bowl driven far below setpoint means a thermostat stuck closed. The temp probe separates it from weak mix, which freezes at a perfectly normal temperature.
symptoms: Left bowl frozen into one solid block.

tests.error-log: AUGER OVERTORQUE trips, repeating since this morning.
tests.temp-probe: Bowl reads −10 °C — far below any setpoint on the dial.
tests.inspect-beater: Bowl frozen into one solid mass around the auger. The auger itself is intact — stalling against the ice, not broken.
tests.continuity-test: Thermostat contacts read closed at every temperature you hold it at.

symptoms: Solid frozen bowl two mornings running, even after staff set it warmer.
Bowl over-freezes overnight; fine for an hour after a manual defrost, then locks up again.

tests.temp-probe: An hour after defrost the bowl is already 6 °C below setpoint and falling.

flavour: It had one job: knowing when to stop.
authenticityNote: A thermostat stuck closed over-freezes the bowl and presents identically to weak mix — frozen block, straining auger. The temp probe is what separates them: stuck stat drives the bowl far below setpoint, weak mix freezes at a perfectly normal temperature.
